auction_style: Alex Tarshis is the most active and analytically inclined trader in the league, having won the 2024 championship. He engages immediately with trade talk ('open for business!'), references auction context in negotiations ('you guys were in on him at the auction'), and seeks value gaps in player comparisons. He's a balanced builder who prioritizes steals/speed and specific positional needs. He names his team 'Let's Deal' — the branding is a philosophy.
price_tendency_detail: Tarshis understands relative value well — he explicitly noted 'I'm not sure there is a significant value gap between Nimmo and Lowe seeing as Nimmo plays the more premium position.' He uses position scarcity as a pricing argument and won't overpay for positionally fungible players.
trade_style: Very active and sophisticated. Tarshis initiates trades frequently, engages every offer, and provides clear reasoning for his positions. He traded multiple times with Chathams across multiple years (including the 2025 Sandy Alcantara/Spencer Steer deal and the 2025 Zebby Matthews/Bryan Reynolds/Realmuto for Yandy Diaz/Williams/Megill 3-for-3). He's comfortable in both buyer and seller roles and uses 'two 7s for a 9' type language showing he thinks in tiers. He also fishes: offered Polanco + Gio for Giancarlo Stanton in 2018 to 'start a dialogue.'
notable_tendencies: Named his team 'Let's Deal' — genuinely lives up to it with consistently high trade volume
2024 league champion — peak performer, likely entered 2026 as the team others want to dethrone
References historical auction prices in trade talks — tracks what players cost at the draft
Thinks in tiers: 'two 7s for a 9 type offer' — sophisticated relative value framing
Speed/SB is a stated priority: 'wouldn't trade any of my guys projected for 15 steals' for Lowe
Joked about saving $13 by saying he 'vehemently disagrees' — has a dry sense of humor
intel_summary: Tarshis is the most sophisticated trader in the league and the reigning champion. He will engage with almost any trade proposal but knows what he wants and prices position scarcity correctly. His focus on speed/SB contributors is a clear drafting signal — expect him to overbid on fast OF with position flexibility. He is harder to fleece than any other owner in the league, so if you're dealing with him, make sure you're getting clear value.
